title: What is a Dietary Pattern?
text:
What is a Dietary Pattern?

Frank Hu, MD, PhD

Professor and Chair

Dept. of Nutrition, HSPH

Professor of Medicine

Harvard Medical School

Conflict of Interest disclosures

пБ∞ None

пВХ пВЯ **Difficult to attribute effects to single dietary components**

пВХ пВЯ **Dietary patterns account for complicated interactions and**

**cumulative effects**

пВХ пВЯ **Dietary patterns are more realistic representations of dietary**

**intake in real life and can be better translated into guidelines**

**Dietary Pattern Analysis: A New Direction in**

**Nutritional Epidemiology**

Frank B. Hu

Current Opinion in Lipidology 2002; 13:3-9

**What is a dietary pattern?**

A dietary pattern describes

the overall diet; the foods,

food groups, and nutrients

included; their

combination and variety;

and the frequency and

quantity with which they

are habitually consumed.

**Components of the Mediterranean Diet high in plant-based foods**

Mediterranean diet and chronic disease prevention

вАҐ The evidence to support health benefits of Mediterranean dietary

pattern is strong

вАҐ There is no single Mediterranean diet because the Mediterranean

region encompasses diverse food cultures and culinary techniques

and styles.

вАҐ The core principles of the Mediterranean diet can be found in other

populations such as traditional Asian and Latin American diets

вАҐ Adaption of these core principles to an individualвАЩs own food

preferences, health conditions, and cultural traditions

вАҐ A comparative analysis of four eating patterns:

вАҐ HEI-2015: Dietary Guidelines for Americans (2015)

вАҐ Mediterranean diet

вАҐ Healthy plant-based diet

вАҐ AHEI: Harvard Healthy Eating Plate

вАҐ Greater adherence to any of the four patterns is associated with 20% lower mortality

вАҐ Healthy eating patterns not only reduce CVD and cancer mortality but also mortality due to

neurological and respiratory diseases

вАҐ These patterns can be tailored to individual food and cultural preferences and health

conditions

**The** **E** **mpirical** **D** **ietary** **I** **nflammatory** **P** **attern (EDIP)**

**_Development_**

_Fred K Tabung, et. al., JN, 2016_

вАҐ We used reduced rank regression + stepwise linear regression

вАҐ To regress 3 biomarkers (CRP, IL6, & TNFa-R2) on 39 pre-defined food groups

вАҐ 18 food groups (9 pro-inflammatory and 9 anti-inflammatory) were selected

**_Validation_**

_Fred K Tabung, et. al. JN, 2017_

_Fred K Tabung, et. al., JN, 2018_

_Jun Li, et al., JACC, 2020_

вАҐ EDIP shows strong

associations with levels of

inflammatory biomarkers

in multiple large

population cohorts

**In 3 large US prospective observational cohorts**

(210,145 participants, 15,837 incident CVD cases during up to 32 years of follow-up),

**higher dietary pro-inflammatory potential (assessed by EDIP) is associated with higher CVD risk**

_Li, J. et al. J Am Coll Cardiol. 2020_

**Foods excluded in different types of dietary patterns**

**21**

вАҐ

Healthy plant-based diets rich in whole grains,

fruits/vegetables, nuts/legumes, oils, coffee/tea

are associated with lower T2D, CVD, and

mortality risk

вАҐ

Unhealthy plant-based diets high in sweetened

beverages, refined grains, potatoes/fries, sweets

are associated with increased risk of chronic

diseases and mortality вАУ worse than animal food-

based diets

вАҐ

These findings underscore the quality of plant

foods in our diet

Satija et al, J Am Coll Cardiol 2017

Satija et al. PLoS Medicine 2016

**Not all plant-based diets are healthy**

Global Diet Quality Score

**Dimensions of a high-quality diet**

вАҐ Provides nutrient adequacy

вАҐ Limits noncommunicable disease (NCD) risk

**Challenges in measuring diet quality**

вАҐ Lack of global standard metrics

вАҐ Global variation in diet patterns and food systems

вАҐ Global variation in burdens of malnutrition

**Characteristics of existing diet quality metrics**

вАҐ Focus on specific dimensions of diet quality or populations

вАҐ Overly complex or simplistic

Measuring Diet Quality

**We developed a Global Diet Quality Score (GDQS) that is:**

вАҐ Entirely food-based (no nutrients involved in scoring)

вАҐ Uses a modestly expanded food list relative to other metrics

вАҐ Incorporates a slightly more complex portion assessment

**These features allow the GDQS to:**

вАҐ Account for foods of nutritional importance globally

вАҐ Predict dimensions of both nutrient adequacy and NCD risk

вАҐ Avoid use of food composition data and keep things simple

In PREDIMED: Stratified by study centers and intervention arms; and adjusted for age, sex,

BMI, smoking, diabetes, dyslipidemia, hypertension, and family history of premature CHD.

In NHS/HPFS: Stratified by study cohorts, sub-studies, and case-control status in original sub-

studies, and adjusted for age, fasting status, and aspirin use at blood draw, BMI, smoking,

physical activity, diabetes, dyslipidemia, hypertension, and family history of premature CHD.

**_Healthy dietary patterns (aMED, HEI-2015, hPDI) in this_**

**_population are associated with a higher abundance of_** **_fi_** **_ber-_**

**_fermenting Clostridia species in the gut_** **_microbiome,_**

**_consistent with previous studies in other racial/ethnic_**

**_groups. Gut microbiota may be involved in the bene_** **_fi_** **_cial_**

**_effect of higher diet quality_** **_on cardiometabolic disease risk._**

40

Conclusions

вАҐ Dietary patterns have become mainstream in nutritional epidemiology and dietary intervention studies

вАҐ Current dietary guidelines have shifted from individual nutrients or foods to overall healthy eating

patterns.

вАҐ There is strong evidence from large cohort studies and RCTs that multiple dietary patterns are

associated with lower risk of chronic diseases and mortality.

вАҐ Diet quality indices can be used to monitor population trends, evaluate the effectiveness of dietary

interventions, and incorporate into policy analyses.

вАҐ Integrating various omics techniques into dietary pattern analyses can help to identify objective

biomarkers and better understand biological mechanisms.

вАҐ Dietary pattern analysis is complementary to rather than replaces traditional nutrient or food analysis.

вАҐ Methods to derive dietary patterns need to more standardized to ensure consistency and comparability

across studies.

вАҐ More studies need to be conducted in populations with diverse SES and race/ethnicity.

41
